lecture_context.lecture_title: What We See and What We Value: AI with the Human Perspective

unit.name: Superhuman perception and the limits of human attention
unit.description: Shows how AI can exceed human recognition granularity and compensate for human attention failures in high-stakes settings.
unit.summary: The second major section starts with fine-grained categorization, arguing that humans are poor at discriminating thousands of similar bird species or car makes/models/years and that this remains a challenging but important vision problem even in the multimodal-LLM era [ts=1454s] [ts=1471s]. Fei-Fei Li describes using fine-grained car recognition over Google Street View data to infer education, income, voting, and environmental patterns, making computer vision a social sensing tool beyond what any human could manually do [ts=1573s] [ts=1623s]. She then pivots to human limitations via the Stroop test and change blindness before linking limited attention to medical errors and to a demo where AI counts surgical items such as gauze to assist clinicians [ts=1656s] [ts=1818s].

key_points.text: Fine-grained categorization asks AI to distinguish very similar categories such as bird species or car make/model/year.
Fine-grained bird recognition still has high error at the most specific taxonomic levels.
Street-view car recognition can be used to study education, income, voting, and environmental patterns.
The Stroop test illustrates conflict between reading words and naming colors as a limit of human attention.
Change blindness shows that humans can miss large scene changes, motivating AI assistance in safety-critical work.
AI item counting in surgery is proposed as a way to reduce dangerous pauses caused by missing tools or gauze.

kp_catalog.name: Fine-grained recognition for superhuman perception
kp_catalog.description: AI can exceed typical human perception by distinguishing extremely similar categories and extracting patterns at scale.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because fine-grained recognition for superhuman perception clarifies aI can exceed typical human perception by distinguishing extremely similar categories and extracting patterns at scale, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: Human attention limits and error-prone perception
kp_catalog.description: Human visual processing has systematic limits such as Stroop interference and change blindness that can create safety risks.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because human attention limits and error-prone perception clarifies human visual processing has systematic limits such as Stroop interference and change blindness that can create safety risks, sharpening later modeling, analysis, or evaluation choices.

kp_catalog.name: AI augmentation over replacement
kp_catalog.description: Smart sensing and machine learning can compensate for labor shortages by supporting monitoring and decision-making in clinical and home settings. The lecture's closing principle is that AI should enhance human capability and care rather than simply replace people.
kp_catalog.importance_rationale: Important because aI augmentation over replacement synthesizes smart sensing and machine learning can compensate for labor shortages by supporting monitoring and decision-making in clinical and home settings into an end-to-end method or integrated recipe.
